Author: deempak
Content: Can i add custom chip art on the top and bottom right corner ( similar to the wafer space logo and qr in the left ) ? Or can i only add inside the pad ring ?

Author: polyfractal
Content: yep, you can! I have a little image on the bottom right of mine. I think the top right may be reserved for an arrow this time around (to help the wirebonding alignment) so I think we can't use that corner

Author: deempak
Content: Hey sure got it thank you much Will use only the bottom right

Author: mole99
Content: You are also free to replace the wafer.space logo on the top left. The precheck only checks the bottom left and the top right, if you have CoB enabled.

Author: polyfractal
Content:
Chinese open source 55nm PDK? https://github.com/openecos-projects/icsprout55-pdk

(Found via https://mp.weixin.qq.com/s/hDV_g7wtZvxg2IIAZ1XvGA)

Author: 246tnt
Content: @BreakingTaps It was mentionned at FSiC last year but ... Not really open. If you look at it there is not much there. You get a standard cell library but only abstract view, no GDS. No DRC rules or any device simulation model.  So you can "assemble" a chip with purely digital design but nothing more, nothing custom, ...

Author: 246tnt
Content: So you can do basically the same you can do with a FPGA.

Author: polyfractal
Content: ah, welp

Author: polyfractal
Content: maybe some day we'll get a 50-65nm open PDK

Author: myyerrol
Content: Next week at FSiC 2026, we'll be introducing the latest updates on the ics55 pdk, eda tools, and design platform, etc. We have also upgraded the ics55 pdk from tier1 to tier 0. Moving forward, we're planning the roadmap, restructuring the repo based on sky130, gfmcu180, ihp130, and will be gradually releasing the remaining files over the next some months.

Author: essen__
Content: Maybe we should indeed re-evaluate your permission to use tools 🤣

Author: mithro_
Content: Are you connected to ics55 in some way?

Author: myyerrol
Content: Yes, we have integrated the ics55 pdk to ECOS Studio, but now it can only generate partial gds because of some key files haven't be released, so we need to do some works (like DRC/LVS/PEX) by ourselves.

Author: myyerrol
Content: I read the IHP's slide at the FSiC 2026, their pdk and open mpw shuttle is fantastic, so we will learn from ihp sg13g2, to port more open-source eda tools to ics55, and release remaining data as soon as possilbe.

Author: myyerrol
Content: And "EuroCDP Open-source PDKS and EDA tooling  recommendations" book also give us great help-a good comparison table.

Author: logic_destroyer
Content:
How hot do your chips get? I'm measuring 35°C on the top surface of the chip.
@Leo Moser (mole99) @Andrew Wingate @BreakingTaps @RebelMike @Tholin @Greg ...

Author: tholin
Content: What clock speed?

Author: logic_destroyer
Content: I hadn't even thought of that. Right now I'm still running at 24 MHz because I'm too lazy to hook up an external clock. But in theory, 35–40 MHz should be achievable.

Author: tholin
Content: 35° is normal at 24MHz

Author: logic_destroyer
Content: @Tholin  Looking at how power scales with frequency, I figure the chip would end up at around 45°C at most. That should still be fine, right?

Author: tholin
Content: Yes

Author: logic_destroyer
Content: thanx

Author: logic_destroyer
Content: I thought the chip might be running a bit hotter because of all the I/O activity.

Author: anfroholic
Content:
Cross posting a message from @Tim Edwards 
> Calling on all analog designers. . . the Chipalooza Challenge is back! Looking for high-quality open source analog circuits that support system-on-chip. This is a 2-year-long project with five individual challenges covering the three major open source PDKs, with the goal of making SoC designs with analog components portable across all of the major open PDKs. It costs nothing but your (valuable) design time, and awards will be given for successful completed circuits. To participate, join the https://fossi-chat.org forum and go to hashtag#Chipalooza for details and updates!
